smartlead_campaign: OR #6 | Personalized Line | Forecast Lead Magnet | Without Other Contact Name

thread.subject: Braids and Wigs ad report
thread.body_text:
Maria, saw braids and wigs leans on same day shipping plus local delivery in about an hour, curious how that's been landing.

 separate note: I made Braids and Wigs a 4-min ad fatigue report from your live Meta ads. the one about to die, the duplicate eating budget, which to scale, and a fresh angle to test.

 most ecom brands in your niche usually run the same handful of ads too long. this spots it before ROAS drops.

 worth a look?

 Kyle H.
 Co-founder | OmniRocket

 P.S. if there's a better person for this, point me their way :)

thread.subject: RE: Braids and Wigs ad report
thread.body_text:
Maria - forgot to mention: the report also catches the ad Meta is quietly throttling. it's usually a near-copy of another ad that's eating budget without you noticing.

 Braids and Wigs's is still sitting ready on my end - want me to send it?

 - Kyle

 P.S. if it's not you, who should I reach out to?

thread.subject: RE: Braids and Wigs ad report
thread.body_text:
Maria - last check on this.

 should I shelve Braids and Wigs's fatigue report, or is there a better week to send it? no worries either way - just don't want to keep landing in your inbox.

 - Kyle

 P.S. if timing's just off, ping me in a few weeks - still happy to send.

Hi Kyle,
 

 Thank you for following up. Before we move forward, could you clarify what you'd need from us, specifically, did you need access to our Meta ad account to put this report together and if so, what level?
 

 Best regards,
 

 
 Maria Mushi 
 Manager – Braids and Wigs 
 London, Ontario 
 226-504-1321 
 www.braidsandwigs.ca

Hi Maria,

 great question, and the short answer is nothing needed on your end for what i already built.

here's the report btw: Braids and Wigs Ad Fatigue Report 

 this report was built entirely from public data. i pulled your live ad from meta's ad library and analyzed them from the outside, so no account access was involved at all.

 access only comes into play for the deeper work: a full audit plus a 30 and 90 day plan mapped to your actual numbers.

 the way we work is we hop on a quick call first, i get to understand your brand and where you're trying to go, and if we're a good fit we move forward. that's the point where i'd need access, and we'd start with read-only.

 worth a 30 min chat to see if it's a fit?

 Book a time here → Calendar 

 - Kyle

copy.tldr: Braids and Wigs is a value-priced Canadian wig and braiding hair shop with a big catalog but only one weak Messenger ad and no reviews, so the play is testing three buyer angles on their higher-priced human hair wigs, not scaling.

deep_dive.what_they_are: A Canadian online beauty shop based in London, Ontario. They sell wigs, braiding hair, extensions, and hair and skin care from many known brands like Sensationnel, Mane Concept, Outre, and Freetress.
deep_dive.what_they_sell: 500 products. Prices run from under a dollar for beads up to about 508 dollars. The median price is 10.52 dollars, which we are using as a stand-in for their average order value since we cannot see their real numbers. The catalog is a mix of cheap add-ons like edge control and wig caps, and higher-priced human hair wigs and bundles.
deep_dive.positioning: High-variety value play. Name brands at low prices, not a boutique premium house. They lean on deals, free shipping, buy now pay later, and fast local delivery.
deep_dive.who_buys: Value-conscious Canadian women who wear protective styles like wigs, braids, weaves, and crochet. They want name-brand looks fast, cheap, and with easy pay-later options.
strengths: Strong, believable offers already in place: free shipping over 69.99, a free gift at 99.99, buy now pay later, and local delivery in London within about an hour.
Big catalog of recognized brands, which gives lots of angles and products to test without needing new inventory.
gaps: Only one live Meta ad, and it is a generic 'Send message' post that lists everything at once instead of selling one clear product to one clear buyer.
No reviews anywhere on the site, so there is no social proof to lean on. That makes cold traffic slower to trust and harder to convert.

game_plan.headline: Test three buyer angles on their glueless human hair wigs to find the one audience and message that converts, before spending a dollar on scale.

phases.goal: Find out which buyer angle and which audience actually buys. We are learning, not scaling. By day 30 we want to know the winning product, the winning message, and a rough cost to get a sale.
campaigns: One sales campaign on Meta with three ad sets, each built around one of the three buyers below. Same product focus, different message and creative per buyer.
One simple retargeting ad set for people who clicked or viewed but did not buy, pointing them back with the free gift at 99.99 as the nudge.
lead_products: Human Hair Water Wave Headband Wig - Glueless and Easy Install (145.12 dollars). Lead with this. Headband wigs are the easiest possible install with zero glue, which matches a value buyer who wants a fast, name-brand look without a salon. It sits above the free shipping and free gift thresholds, so it pulls the average order up. We are guessing it is a strong seller because it is featured near the top of the catalog and hits the 'quick, easy, no skill needed' promise their buyer wants.

Human Hair Kinky Headband Wig - Glueless and Easy Install (123.35 dollars). Run as the second product test. Same easy-install promise, different texture, so we can see which look the audience responds to.

Sensationnel Empire Human Hair Feather Crochet Braids - Ripple Wave 14 inch (58.05 dollars). A lower-priced option for the protective-style crowd who braid rather than wear wigs. Good for the third buyer angle.
phases.creative: Short video or simple before-and-after style images showing the glueless headband wig going on fast. Lean on their own customer words: 'bring beauty to life without the huge price tag', 'perfect hairline', 'extra hold'. Each ad names one buyer's reason to buy. Put the offer in plain sight: free shipping over 69.99, free gift at 99.99, and buy now pay later so the price feels smaller. Do NOT claim the hair repairs, heals, or regrows anything.
phases.budget: Start at 50 to 60 dollars a day across the three ad sets. That is enough to get real click and add-to-cart data on which angle works within 30 days, without burning money before we know anything.
phases.what_success_looks_like: By day 30 we can point to one buyer angle and one product that clearly beat the others on cost per add-to-cart and cost per sale. That becomes the base we would scale in month two. We also expect the retargeting ad to bring back a chunk of the people who clicked but hesitated.

estimates.label: Starting budget
estimates.value: 50 to 60 dollars a day, about 1,500 to 1,800 dollars for the month
estimates.basis: Enough to get roughly 30 to 40 clicks a day at typical costs in this category, split across three angles, which is the floor for learning which one works in 30 days.

estimates.label: Revenue we would aim to add
estimates.value: We are not promising a revenue number for month one
estimates.basis: Month one is a learning budget. With no view of their current sales, spend, or return, and no reviews to help conversion, any revenue figure would be a guess. We would set a real target once we see which angle converts and at what cost.

estimates.label: Realistic average order to plan around
estimates.value: Aim to push orders toward 70 to 100 dollars
estimates.basis: Their median product price is only 10.52 dollars, which is too low to carry paid ads on its own. Leading with 120 to 145 dollar human hair wigs and their 99.99 free gift threshold pulls the order value up to where paid ads can work.

assumptions: Their real average order value is higher than the 10.52 dollar median because customers buy wigs and bundles, not just beads and gel. If most orders are actually tiny add-ons, paid ads will struggle and the plan changes.
The glueless headband wigs are among their better sellers. If Maria says something else sells best, we lead with that instead.
Their checkout, shipping, and buy now pay later all work smoothly. If checkout is broken, no ad will fix it.
They have enough margin on the human hair wigs to pay for ads and still profit. We need to confirm this on the call.

questions.question: Are you spending anything on Meta ads right now, and if so, roughly how much a month?
questions.listening_for: We only saw one live ad. If they spend almost nothing, this is a fresh start. If they spend a lot with little to show, the problem is setup, not budget. A blank stare here means the account may be barely touched.

questions.question: Who runs your ads today, you, a staff member, or another agency?
questions.listening_for: If Maria does it herself between other jobs, that explains the single generic ad. If another agency runs it, find out what they promised and why she is looking around.

questions.question: What is your real average order value, and what does a typical order look like, one wig or a basket of small items?
questions.listening_for: This is the single most important number. The 10.52 dollar median scares me. If real orders are 80 to 120 dollars because of wigs, ads can work. If most orders are tiny, they cannot afford paid traffic yet.

questions.question: On a wig that sells for around 130 dollars, roughly how much is left after you pay for the product?
questions.listening_for: Whether there is enough margin to pay for ads and still make money. If margin is thin, we lean harder on repeat buyers and average order value, not just new sales.

questions.question: When someone messages your one ad instead of buying on the site, what usually happens next?
questions.listening_for: Their only ad uses 'Send message'. If messages turn into sales, great, but that does not scale well. If they mostly go nowhere, we are leaking buyers who could have checked out directly.

questions.question: You have no reviews on the site. Do you have any happy customer photos, texts, or messages we could use?
questions.listening_for: This is the biggest conversion gap. Even screenshots of customer messages or install photos give us social proof for the ads. If she has none at all, we build a quick way to collect them first.

questions.question: Which products actually sell best for you, and which ones do you make the most on?
questions.listening_for: Confirms or corrects my guess that the glueless headband wigs lead. Best seller and best margin are not always the same. We want to advertise the overlap.

questions.question: How much of your business is local London delivery versus shipping across Canada?
questions.listening_for: If local is a big chunk, we can run cheap local-radius ads on the 'delivered in an hour' promise, which is a real edge. If it is tiny, we focus on Canada-wide.

questions.question: When people land on the site, do they add to cart and then drop off, or not add at all?
questions.listening_for: Tells us if the problem is traffic quality or the site itself. Lots of add-to-cart but no purchase points at checkout, shipping cost, or trust. No add-to-cart points at the product page or price.

questions.question: Do you get any sales from Google or people searching your brand and product names now?
questions.listening_for: People clearly search for wigs, braiding hair, and named brands like Sensationnel and Outre. That is a real search opening. If she already gets free Google traffic, paid search and SEO could add sales on top of Meta. This is a month-two conversation, not month one.

questions.question: Are you the only shop in London doing fast local wig and braid delivery, or are there others?
questions.listening_for: If she owns 'same day wig delivery in London', that is a search term worth ranking for locally. A local SEO and Google angle could be cheap and high intent. Only push this if she confirms the local demand is real.

objections.objection: My prices are already low, I do not have much margin left to pay for ads.
objections.answer: That is exactly why we do not lead with the 10 dollar items. We lead with the 120 to 145 dollar human hair wigs where there is room to pay for a sale and still profit, and we use your free gift at 99.99 to push orders up. Month one is a small learning budget, not a big bet.

objections.objection: I already tried ads and they did not really work.
objections.answer: The one ad we can see is a general 'message us' post that lists everything at once. That is a hard way to sell. It does not name a product or a buyer. We would test three clear buyers against three clear products so you can see which one actually pays, instead of guessing.

objections.objection: Why should I pay an agency instead of just boosting posts myself?
objections.answer: Boosting sends money to whoever likes your page already. What we do is find which new buyer group is worth the spend and which product wins, then put money behind only that. The report you already got was built from your public ad, no account access, so you have seen how we work before spending anything.

objections.objection: I do not have reviews or fancy content, will ads even work?
objections.answer: That is a real gap and worth fixing first. Even screenshots of customer messages or a few install photos give us proof to use in the ads. Part of month one is helping you gather that so cold buyers trust you faster.

red_flags.flag: The median product price is only 10.52 dollars.
red_flags.why_it_matters: If real orders are that small, paid ads cannot pay for themselves. The whole plan depends on customers actually buying the higher-priced wigs and bundles. We must confirm the real average order value before promising anything.

red_flags.flag: No reviews or social proof anywhere on the site.
red_flags.why_it_matters: Cold buyers seeing an ad for a 130 dollar wig from a shop they do not know need proof it is legit. With zero reviews, conversion will be slower and cost per sale higher until we fix it.

red_flags.flag: Their only live ad uses 'Send message' as the call to action.
red_flags.why_it_matters: This pushes people into chat instead of checkout. It is hard to track and hard to scale. If most of their current selling happens by message, moving to direct website sales is a change they need to be ready for.

red_flags.flag: Maria asked what account access we need before agreeing to anything.
red_flags.why_it_matters: She is cautious about access and control. Kyle should reassure her the report needed nothing, and access starts read-only only if they move forward. Do not push hard on access early.

other_channel_openings: People actively search for wigs, braiding hair, and named brands like Sensationnel, Outre, and Freetress. That is real search demand, so Google Shopping and search ads could add high-intent sales on top of Meta once the Meta test proves the winning products.

Fast local wig and braid delivery in London, Ontario is a specific local search angle. If demand is real, local SEO and a Google Business presence could be cheap and high intent. Confirm the local volume before leaning in.
